Atomless decomposition (Sierpinski/Lyapunov splitting): an atomless probability measure splits into M probability pieces with prescribed positive convex weights and pairwise disjoint supports (mu = sum alpha_k P_k). MACHINE-CHECKED (fully de-axiomatised): the pieces are normalized restrictions (alpha_k)^-1 mu.restrict(A_k) to a prescribed-mass disjoint partition carved by iterating the now-proved Sierpinski IVT primitive (sierpinski-ivt); the partition and probability-normalization scaffolding is machine-checked (Foundations.exists_probability_decomposition). Positive weights (alpha_k != 0) are assumed so each piece is a genuine probability measure. #print axioms lists only propext/Classical.choice/Quot.sound. Soundness fix: the earlier per-piece hemisphere clause was inconsistent at M=1 and was dropped; prop_2_2 now acquires the hemisphere dynamically per piece via lemma_3_2.

Disentanglement dynamics output (Section 3.3): one schedule concentrates each measure into a small ball B(alpha_i, r), r<1, around a unit direction alpha_i, with directions pairwise 2r-separated. The concrete geometric output of Lemmas 3.2-3.4 run as the Section 3.3 induction (leaf L11 the geometric core); rests on the missing continuity-equation theory.

Structural flow algebra (Axioms/Dynamics.lean). FULLY DE-AXIOMATIZED (milestone M3 complete). measureFlow is DEFINED as the pushforward of the point flow map (measureFlow theta t mu := mu.map (flowMap theta t)), so measureFlow_map is definitional (rfl) and the measure-level semigroup laws measureFlow_comp / measureFlow_id / measureFlow_inv are DERIVED. The schedule algebra is concrete: Params d = List (Block d), idParams = [], comp = List.append, switches = List.length, so switches_comp / switches_id are DERIVED. The remaining point-level facts, formerly axioms, are now THEOREMS discharged against the concrete Foundations flowMap (the fold of the per-block characteristic flows): flowMap_id = flowMap_nil, flowMap_comp = flowMap_append, flowMap_inv = flowMap_reverseNeg_comp (with inv corrected to reverse-AND-negate), flowMap_bijective, flowMap_lipschitz, flowMap_id_on_parked. #print axioms on all of these lists only propext/Classical.choice/Quot.sound. Nothing in the flow/continuity-equation machinery is axiomatised any more; see flow-axiom-flip.

Parking / simultaneous action (Appendix B): if a family of measures has pairwise disjoint supports and each member can be steered to within eps of its target by some schedule, a single schedule steers all of them simultaneously (each gated to its region, parking on the others).

Convexity of W2 under mixtures: for probability measures with shared weights, W2(sum a_k P_k, sum a_k Q_k) is at most the uniform bound on the component distances (gluing of couplings, W2(mix)^2 <= sum a_k W2(P_k,Q_k)^2). Standard optimal-transport fact, absent from Mathlib.
introduce the labeled axiom layer for the missing Mathlib theory

Measure continuity along a shrinking sublevel set (milestone M4, lemma_B_2 slice 1: eq. B.6). MACHINE-CHECKED. exists_closed_sublevel_mass_ge: for a continuous f and a measure with finite mass on the open sublevel {f < R}, any fraction 1-eps < 1 of that mass is already carried by a closed sublevel {f <= r} with r < R. Applied to f = d_g(z, .) this is the cap estimate the B.2 retention argument needs, mu(B(z,r)) >= (1-eps) mu(B(z,R)). Proof: continuity of measure from below (tendsto_measure_iUnion_atTop) along the increasing closed sublevels A n = {f <= R - 1/(n+1)}, whose union is the open sublevel; the target fraction is strictly below the finite limit (ENNReal.mul_lt_mul_right + ENNReal.sub_lt_self), hence eventually attained. #print axioms clean (propext/Classical.choice/Quot.sound). First slice of the lemma_B_2 discharge (M4); the hard cores -- the cutoff Block instantiation of the ReLU-gated field and the finite-time logistic reaching estimate (B.7) -- remain. Also flags a fidelity issue: the lemma_B_2 axiom is stated for arbitrary sets B0/B1, but B.2 requires geodesic balls, so a faithful discharge must restrict the signature.

Flow algebra of an autonomous Lipschitz field (milestone M3, Phase 1 completion). MACHINE-CHECKED. Mathlib has local Picard-Lindelof, global uniqueness (ODE_solution_unique_univ), and the Gronwall trajectory bound (dist_le_of_trajectories_ODE), but NO global-existence continuation and NO constructor turning a Lipschitz vector field into a Flow object (only Flow.id / Flow.fromIter). Rather than fabricate/axiomatize the Flow object, we prove the flow PROPERTIES the paper's construction uses, each for a given integral curve of the autonomous field v == V: integralCurve_unique (two curves agreeing at 0 are equal -- injectivity behind flowMap_bijective), integralCurve_dist_le (dist(g1 t)(g2 t) <= dist(g1 0)(g2 0) e^{K t} for t>=0 -- the Lipschitz-in-initial-value estimate behind flowMap_lipschitz; NO axioms at all, fully constructive), integralCurve_eq_of_field_zero (field vanishing at x => the curve through x is constant -- the Parked / flowMap_id_on_parked content), integralCurve_comp_add + integralCurve_semigroup (eta t = gamma(s+t) -- the flowMap_comp semigroup law Phi^{s+t}=Phi^t o Phi^s). These are the genuine mathematical content of the four flow axioms; the only remaining gap is the (missing-in-Mathlib) global-existence packaging that turns 'for a given curve' into 'for the flow map'. #print axioms clean. Does not yet discharge a paper flow axiom (that needs global existence + the mean-field coupling, M3 Phase 4).

The continuity-equation flow-axiom flip (milestone M3 COMPLETE). MACHINE-CHECKED. Axioms/ContinuityEquation.lean and Axioms/Dynamics.lean formerly axiomatised the entire flow layer: an opaque Block type, an opaque flowMap, and the structural axioms flowMap_id / flowMap_comp / flowMap_inv / flowMap_lipschitz / flowMap_bijective / Parked / flowMap_id_on_parked. These are now ALL discharged onto the concrete Foundations flowMap (the fold of per-block Picard-Lindelof characteristic flows). Mechanism: `export MeasureToMeasure (Block flowMap Params switches)` makes the Axioms names ALIASES of the concrete Foundations declarations (same underlying decl, so no name collision in consumers that open both namespaces), and each former axiom becomes a theorem -- flowMap_id := flowMap_nil, flowMap_comp := flowMap_append, flowMap_inv := flowMap_reverseNeg_comp (with inv REDEFINED from the unsound List.reverse to reverse-and-negate (theta.map Block.neg).reverse), flowMap_bijective, flowMap_lipschitz (all-t via time-reversal, no 0<=t threading), Parked := every block's field vanishes on S, flowMap_id_on_parked := flowMap_fixed_of_forall_field_zero. Effect on the headline theorems: #print axioms theorem_1_1 / theorem_1_2 no longer lists ANY flow/Block/Parked/continuity-equation axiom -- they now rest only on Axioms.W2 (M2 track) and the still-informal mid-level lemmas (cluster_to_point, exists_disentangling_balls, exists_parked_schedule, lemma_5_4, W2_map_le_L2). #print axioms on every flipped theorem lists only propext/Classical.choice/Quot.sound. The full audit (axiom-report + claimgraph audit + reconcile + sorry-sweep) passes.

Per-block characteristic-flow existence (milestone M3, Phase 2: closing the global-existence gap the flow algebra flagged). MACHINE-CHECKED. Introduces the concrete Block d structure (replacing the opaque axiom Block d : Type): a globally-Lipschitz (lipschitz : LipschitzWith lipConst field), globally-bounded (field_le : forall x, ||field x|| <= bound), radially-tangent (radial : <x, field x> = gate x * (||x||^2 - 1), with |2 gate| <= gateBound) velocity field plus a nonnegative duration. The paper's ReLU-gated projected field P_x^perp((cos R - <z,x>)_+ . omega) (B.5) and barycenter field P_x^perp(<E_mu[x],alpha> . alpha) (B.9) are instances after a cutoff off the sphere makes them globally Lipschitz+bounded (the raw quadratic projector is only locally Lipschitz). Block.isPicardLindelof: the block's autonomous field satisfies IsPicardLindelof on [-T,T] around any point x0, for any radius r -- via IsPicardLindelof.of_time_independent with ball radius a = bound*T + r, where GLOBAL boundedness keeps the well-posedness constant from growing with the radius so the interval condition L*T <= a-r holds with equality. This sidesteps the missing global-existence continuation the flow-algebra claim flagged: boundedness on a fixed time interval is enough. Block.exists_integralCurveOn: for any T>=0 there is a curve alpha with alpha 0 = x0 solving x-dot = field x on [-T,T] (from IsPicardLindelof.exists_eq_forall_mem_Icc_hasDerivWithinAt). #print axioms clean (propext/Classical.choice/Quot.sound). Reusable infrastructure; the total flow map (fold over blocks) and the paper flow-axiom flip are the next M3 slices.

Global point flow of a block, by gluing the interval solutions (milestone M3, Phase 2: the global-existence core the SphereFlow foundation explicitly deferred as missing from Mathlib). MACHINE-CHECKED. Block.integralCurve_eqOn (interval uniqueness): two solutions of x-dot = field x on Ioo a c agreeing at a point of the interval agree throughout it (ODE_solution_unique_of_mem_Ioo with the state set s == univ, using the block's global Lipschitzness). Block.exists_globalIntegralCurve: for every x there is a curve Phi with Phi 0 = x and IsIntegralCurve Phi (fun _ => field) on ALL of R. Construction: from Block.exists_integralCurveOn take, for each n, a solution alpha_n through x on the open interval (-n,n) (upgrading the closed-interval HasDerivWithinAt to HasDerivAt at interior points via Icc_mem_nhds); coherence hcoh (from integralCurve_eqOn) makes the family consistent -- for m <= n, alpha_m = alpha_n on (-m,m); define Phi t := alpha_{N t} t with N t := ceil|t| + 1 > |t|, so on the open neighbourhood (-(N t), N t) of t the total curve agrees with alpha_{N t} (two-way coherence by le_total on the indices), giving IsIntegralCurveAt via IsIntegralCurveOn.isIntegralCurveAt + HasDerivAt.congr_of_eventuallyEq, hence IsIntegralCurve by isIntegralCurve_iff_isIntegralCurveAt. This is the global-in-time existence that turns 'for a given curve' into 'for the flow map' -- exactly the gap flow-algebra-integral-curve flagged. #print axioms clean (propext/Classical.choice/Quot.sound). Next: the total flow map (blockFlow via choice + Lipschitz/measurable dependence, fold over the schedule) and the paper flow-axiom flip.

The point flow map of a block, with its full one-parameter-group algebra (milestone M3, Phase 3). MACHINE-CHECKED. blockFlow b t x := (the global integral curve through x, from exists_globalIntegralCurve) evaluated at t -- the point transport Phi_b^t. Properties, all derived from the SphereFlow.lean autonomous-field algebra: blockFlow_zero / blockFlow_zero_eq_id (Phi^0 = id); blockFlow_add (SEMIGROUP: Phi^t (Phi^s x) = Phi^{s+t} x, from integralCurve_semigroup); blockFlow_injective (two curves agreeing at time t agree at 0, via integralCurve_comp_add + integralCurve_unique); blockFlow_bijective + blockFlow_neg_comp (bijective with inverse Phi^{-t}, so Phi^{-t} o Phi^t = id); blockFlow_mem_sphere (SPHERE INVARIANCE for t>=0: the radial-tangency identity radial + the uniform gate bound feed norm_sq_eq_one_of_radial_tangent); blockFlow_dist_le + lipschitzWith_blockFlow + continuous_blockFlow + measurable_blockFlow (Gronwall dependence on the initial value dist(Phi^t x)(Phi^t y) <= dist x y e^{K t} for t>=0, hence Phi^t is Lipschitz/continuous/measurable -- what the pushforward measureFlow needs). #print axioms clean (propext/Classical.choice/Quot.sound). This is the complete single-block point flow; the total flow map is the fold of these over a schedule, and then the paper flow-axiom flip (with inv corrected to reverse-and-negate).

The schedule flow map, folded over the block list (milestone M3, Phase 3). MACHINE-CHECKED. flowMap theta t := theta.foldr (fun b acc => acc o b.blockFlow t) id -- run each block for time t, earlier blocks applied first. Structural laws by list induction over the single-block point-flow facts: flowMap_nil (flowMap [] t = id, the identity schedule), flowMap_cons, flowMap_append (COMPOSITION = concatenation: flowMap (theta1 ++ theta2) t = flowMap theta2 t o flowMap theta1 t -- the flowMap_comp content), flowMap_bijective (composition of bijective point flows), measurable_flowMap (composition of measurable point flows, for t>=0 -- what measureFlow's pushforward needs), flowMap_mem_sphere (preserves the sphere, for t>=0). #print axioms clean (propext/Classical.choice/Quot.sound). This is the concrete flowMap the Axioms/ContinuityEquation + Axioms/Dynamics axioms will be flipped onto; the remaining piece is time-reversal (inv corrected to reverse-and-negate, so flowMap_inv holds for a genuine flow) and the flip itself.

Time-reversal of the schedule flow map (milestone M3, Phase 3, soundness correction). MACHINE-CHECKED. The reversed schedule must ALSO negate each block's field: reversing the block order alone gives flow-for-2t, not the identity, so the naive inv := reverse is FALSE for a genuine flow (a fidelity bug the concretization surfaced, like the M8a hemisphere collapse). Block.neg negates the field and the radial gate (staying a valid Block: same Lipschitz/bound/gate-bound constants, radial identity via inner_neg_right). Block.blockFlow_neg: b.neg.blockFlow t x = b.blockFlow (-t) x (the reversed block's forward flow is the original's backward flow -- proved by the chain rule s |-> blockCurve x (-s) is the integral curve of -field, then integralCurve_unique). flowMap_reverseNeg_comp: flowMap ((theta.map Block.neg).reverse) t o flowMap theta t = id -- the reverse-and-negate schedule genuinely inverts the flow, by list induction using blockFlow_neg + blockFlow_neg_comp (Phi^{-t} o Phi^t = id). #print axioms clean (propext/Classical.choice/Quot.sound). Completes the Foundations flow machinery; the paper flow-axiom flip (Axioms/ContinuityEquation + Dynamics onto these, with inv redefined to reverse-and-negate) is the remaining step.

The gated cutoff Block instantiation of the ReLU-gated field (milestone M4, lemma_B_2 slice 2 COMPLETE). MACHINE-CHECKED. gatedBlock builds a genuine well-posed Block d from the Appendix B.5 characteristic V(x) = chi(||x||) (<z,x> - cos R)_+ P_x^perp omega, for unit z, omega and threshold cos R >= -1, over any duration T >= 0. Construction: gatedField z omega cosR x = gateFactor z cosR x . tangentialProjector x omega, where gateFactor = normCutoff . reluGate (reluGate x = max 0 (<z,x> - cosR), globally 1-Lipschitz via Cauchy-Schwarz + the 1-Lipschitz clamp max 0, bounded by 3 on the ball of radius 2). All Block obligations discharged: gatedField_lipschitz (globally 32-Lipschitz, via lipschitzWith_smul_of_vanishing with s=gateFactor [Ks=4,Bs=3,vanishing outside ball 2] and V=projector [on-ball Kv=4 via the split <y,omega>y-<x,omega>x = <y,omega>(y-x)+(<y,omega>-<x,omega>)x, on-ball Bv=5]); gatedField_norm_le (bounded by 15); gatedField_radial (<x,V(x)> = c(x)(||x||^2-1) with gate c(x) = -gateFactor x . <x,omega>, holding for ALL x by a global computation since the field is gateFactor.(true projector) everywhere -- the cutoff tames only the Lipschitz estimate, not the algebra, so NO case split); abs_two_gatedGate_le (|2c(x)| <= 12). #print axioms gatedBlock clean (propext/Classical.choice/Quot.sound). Completes lemma_B_2 slice 2; only slice 4 (schedule assembly + measure/preimage + restricting the axiom signature from arbitrary sets to geodesic balls) remains for the M4 discharge.

Reusable Lipschitz gluing infrastructure for the cutoff Block (milestone M4, lemma_B_2 slice 2a). MACHINE-CHECKED. The gated characteristic V_theta(x) = chi(||x||) (<z,x> - cos R)_+ P_x^perp omega has a tangential-projector factor P_x^perp omega = omega - <x,omega> x that is QUADRATIC in x, hence not globally Lipschitz; assembling a genuine Block (which needs a globally Lipschitz, bounded field) requires field-independent facts, proved here. lipschitzWith_smul: a BOUNDED-Lipschitz product rule -- if f (scalar) and V (vector) are each Lipschitz AND bounded then x |-> f x . V x is Lipschitz with constant Bf*KV + Kf*BV, via the telescoping split f x.(V x - V y) + (f x - f y).V y. normCutoff: the explicit 1-Lipschitz cutoff chi(x) = max 0 (min 1 (2 - ||x||)), equal to 1 on the closed unit ball, 0 outside the ball of radius 2, range [0,1]. lipschitzWith_smul_of_vanishing (the crux, any normed codomain F): a compactly-supported bounded-Lipschitz scalar s (vanishing outside the ball of radius R) times a vector field V that is bounded (Bv) and Lipschitz (Kv) ONLY ON that ball is globally (Bs*Kv+Ks*Bv)-Lipschitz. The proof splits on which of x,y lies in the support; when the far point has s=0 the difference collapses to one term whose coefficient is controlled by |s x| = |s x - s y| <= Ks||x-y|| -- so the vanishing hypothesis does double duty (zeroes the far term AND bounds the near coefficient), avoiding any boundary-point/IVT gluing. #print axioms clean (propext/Classical.choice/Quot.sound).

The gated flow avoids the poles, keeping the logistic coordinate in (-1,1) (milestone M4, lemma_B_2 discharge step 2). MACHINE-CHECKED. The poles +-omega are fixed points of the gated field: gatedField_pole_eq_zero (tangentialProjector omega omega = 0) and gatedField_neg_pole_eq_zero (P_{-omega} omega = omega - <-omega,omega>(-omega) = omega - omega = 0). blockFlow_ne_pole / blockFlow_ne_neg_pole: for any block whose field is gatedField, the flow from x != omega (resp. x != -omega) never reaches omega (resp. -omega) -- by uniqueness, since blockFlow fixes the pole (Block.blockFlow_fixed) and blockFlow t is injective. inner_gatedFlow_mem_Ioo: combining with the strict Cauchy-Schwarz inner_mem_Ioo_of_ne, for x on the sphere with x != +-omega the logistic coordinate u(t) = <Phi_t x, omega> lies in (-1,1) for EVERY t >= 0 -- exactly the range hypothesis logistic_flow_reach requires, now supplied along the whole trajectory rather than assumed. #print axioms inner_gatedFlow_mem_Ioo clean (propext/Classical.choice/Quot.sound). Step 2 of the lemma_B_2 dynamical discharge; together with gated-flow-ode (step 1) it furnishes the ODE + range hypotheses of logistic_flow_reach for the gated flow.

The gated flow satisfies the logistic gate ODE (milestone M4, lemma_B_2 discharge step 1). MACHINE-CHECKED. Connects the concrete gatedBlock (Foundations/GatedBlock.lean) to the gate ODE leaf L2 (Leaves/GateODE.lean). hasDerivAt_inner_gatedFlow: for any block b whose field is gatedField z omega cosR, along the characteristic flow Phi_t from a sphere point x the coordinate u(t) = <Phi_t x, omega> obeys u'(t) = gateFactor(Phi_t x)(1 - u(t)^2) (eq. B.5), for every t >= 0. Proof: the gated field is exactly the tangential projection of the scaled drift gateFactor x . omega (gatedField_eq_projector_smul, via tangentialProjector_smul), the flow stays on the sphere (Block.blockFlow_mem_sphere), and the integral-curve derivative (Block.blockCurve_isIntegralCurve) feeds directly into gate_hasDerivAt_inner. gatedBlock_hasDerivAt_inner specializes this to the canonical gatedBlock. First step of the lemma_B_2 dynamical discharge, wiring the block (slice 2) to the ODE crux (slice 3).

Finite-time reaching of the self-centered gated flow (milestone M4, lemma_B_2 discharge step 3: eq. B.7 dynamical core). MACHINE-CHECKED. gatedBlock_reach: for the block centered at its own drift target (z = omega), from a sphere point x (x != +-omega) the gated flow drives the coordinate <Phi_T x, omega> to any target level b < 1, provided T is large enough that the log-odds budget logOdds b <= logOdds <x,omega> + 2(<x,omega> - cosR)T is met -- i.e. Phi_T x lands in the sub-cap {y | b <= <y,omega>} of omega. This is the gated flow CONTRACTING A CAP TOWARD ITS CENTER, the essential dynamical content of B.7. Proof assembles all prior pieces with no circularity: the coordinate u(t) = <Phi_t x, omega> obeys the gate ODE u' = g(1-u^2) (hasDerivAt_inner_gatedFlow) and stays in (-1,1) (inner_gatedFlow_mem_Ioo); it is MONOTONE non-decreasing since u' >= 0 (gate g = gateFactor >= 0 always, and 1-u^2 > 0), via monotoneOn_of_deriv_nonneg; hence u(t) >= u(0), so on the sphere (normCutoff = 1) the gate g(t) = (u(t) - cosR)_+ >= u(0) - cosR = c0 stays uniformly bounded below -- the vanishing of the cutoff never bites and the gate lower bound needs no circular assumption. Feeding the ODE + range + gate-bound into logistic_flow_reach yields the reaching. Nontrivial precisely in the active region cosR < <x,omega> (c0 > 0). #print axioms gatedBlock_reach clean (propext/Classical.choice/Quot.sound). Step 3 of the lemma_B_2 dynamical discharge; only the B.8 measure-preimage step (turning point-reaching into (1-eps) mass retention) remains.

Geodesic convexity on the sphere (milestone M5, foundations). MACHINE-CHECKED. A subset s of the unit sphere S^{d-1} is geodesically convex (GeodesicConvex) when it is closed under normalized positive chords: for x,y in s and a,b>0, the point ||a*x+b*y||^-1 * (a*x+b*y) lies in s. On an open hemisphere these normalized chords are exactly the minimizing geodesic arcs, so this is the faithful spherical analogue of ordinary convexity (pure inner-product characterization, no arccos). Key lemma geodesicConvex_open_hemisphere: an open spherical hemisphere {x in S | 0 < <e,x>} is geodesically convex -- the fact the paper uses for the orthant/hemisphere confinement (Lemma 3.2). Also: singletons are geodesically convex, and geodesic convexity is closed under arbitrary (nonempty-indexed) intersection, so an orthant (intersection of hemispheres) is geodesically convex. Mathlib has Convex/ConvexCone/SameRay but no geodesic convexity, so this is built in-repo. #print axioms clean. First slice toward the disentanglement geometry behind exists_disentangling_balls; generalizes leaf L11 (barycenter_noncolinear_of_disjoint_hull).

The geodesic hull is geodesically convex (milestone M5, hull bridge). MACHINE-CHECKED. Connects leaf L11's geodesicHull s = cone(s) cap S^{d-1} to the GeodesicConvex predicate: geodesicConvex_geodesicHull -- if every generator of s lies strictly on e's side (0 < <e,p>, i.e. s is in the open hemisphere of e), then geodesicHull s is geodesically convex. This is the 'hull = cone cap sphere is geodesic-convex' characterization the paper asserts when it calls conv_g geodesic-convex. Also geodesicHull_subset_hemisphere: the hull sits inside the open hemisphere of e. Together with geodesicConvex_open_hemisphere this gives the exact geometric picture behind Section 3.3 disentanglement (disjoint hulls inside a common hemisphere). Supporting facts inConicalSpan.add (cone closed under addition) and inner_pos_of_inConicalSpan (a nonzero conical point in e's hemisphere has 0 < <e,.>) proved too. #print axioms clean.

Separating-hyperplane criterion for geodesic-hull disjointness (milestone M5). MACHINE-CHECKED. geodesicHull_disjoint_of_separated: if a direction e is strictly positive on every generator of s1 and strictly negative on every generator of s2, then geodesicHull s1 and geodesicHull s2 are disjoint (any common point would have <e,.> both positive and negative). Mirror lemma inner_neg_of_inConicalSpan. Corollary barycenter_noncolinear_of_separated composes this with leaf L11 (barycenter_noncolinear_of_disjoint_hull) to get non-colinear barycenters from a separating hyperplane. This is the clean sufficient condition the Section 3.3 disentanglement uses to make two clusters' hulls disjoint. #print axioms clean.

The geodesic hull is the smallest geodesically convex set (milestone M5, minimality). MACHINE-CHECKED. geodesicHull_subset_of_geodesicConvex: if C is geodesically convex and contains every point of s, then geodesicHull s subset of C. This is the universal property that justifies calling geodesicHull a hull. Proof by Finset.induction (normalize_conical_mem): split off one generator p0; the remaining sub-combination u, once nonzero, normalizes into C by the inductive hypothesis, and normalize(w*p0 + u) = normalize(w*p0 + ||u||*normalize(u)) is a normalized positive chord of p0 in C and normalize(u) in C, hence in C by geodesic convexity. Companions: geodesicHull_mono (monotone in the generating set), mem_geodesicHull_self (each unit generator is in its hull), inConicalSpan.mono. #print axioms clean. Completes the hull characterization side of M5 (hull = smallest geodesic-convex set = cone cap sphere).

The finite-time logistic reaching estimate (milestone M4, lemma_B_2 slice 3: eq. B.5-B.7, the ODE crux). MACHINE-CHECKED. The gated characteristic obeys the scalar logistic ODE u' = g(1-u^2) (u = <x,omega>, gate g >= 0, leaf L2 gate_hasDerivAt_inner); on the active region g >= c0 > 0 the flow drives u toward 1 (x toward omega). Key device: the log-odds substitution logOdds s = log((1+s)/(1-s)) whose derivative along the flow is EXACTLY 2g (hasDerivAt_logOdds gives logOdds' = 2/(1-s^2); composed with u' = g(1-u^2) the 1-u^2 cancels), so logOdds(u) grows at rate >= 2c0. logistic_flow_logOdds_le: logOdds(u T) >= logOdds(u 0) + 2c0 T (monotoneOn_of_deriv_nonneg applied to F = logOdds(u) - 2c0 t). logistic_flow_reach: since logOdds is increasing on (-1,1), for any target b < 1 with logOdds b <= logOdds(u 0) + 2c0 T we get u T >= b -- the flow reaches the cap. The range u in (-1,1) is a hypothesis (supplied by the geometry, strict Cauchy-Schwarz for distinct unit vectors, not the ODE). #print axioms clean (propext/Classical.choice/Quot.sound). This is the make-or-break dynamical core of lemma_B_2 (B.7); it de-risks the whole M4 discharge. Remaining: the cutoff Block instantiation (slice 2) and the measure/preimage assembly (slice 4).

Mass retention under a set-mapping (milestone M4, lemma_B_2 discharge step 4: the measure-theoretic half of B.8). MACHINE-CHECKED. le_measureFlow_of_mapsTo: if the flow at time T >= 0 maps a set S into a measurable set A (Set.MapsTo (flowMap theta T) S A), then the transported measure of A is at least the original measure of S: mu S <= measureFlow theta T mu A. This is the bridge that turns a POINT-level reaching statement (every point of S flows into A -- supplied by gatedBlock_reach) into a MASS statement, via the pushforward definition measureFlow = mu.map (flowMap theta T), Measure.map_apply (using measurable_flowMap), and measure_mono on the preimage S subset (flowMap theta T)^{-1} A. #print axioms le_measureFlow_of_mapsTo clean (propext/Classical.choice/Quot.sound). Sierpinski's intermediate-value theorem for nonatomic measures (subset form). MACHINE-CHECKED (was an axiom, now fully proved). Real-line case (exists_measurableSet_subset_measure_eq_real): for a finite atomless measure mu on R, a measurable set E, and any target r <= mu E, there is a measurable subset F of E with mu F = r -- proved directly, the crux being that t |-> (mu(E cap Iic t)).toReal is continuous because its increment over [0,t] is the Bochner primitive int_0^t 1_E dmu (intervalIntegral.continuous_primitive, holds precisely because mu has NoAtoms), so it runs from 0 (t -> -inf, tendsto_measure_iInter_atBot) to (mu E).toReal (t -> +inf, tendsto_measure_iUnion_atTop) and the intermediate value theorem (intermediate_value_Icc) attains r.toReal at some t0; take F = E cap Iic t0. Standard-Borel case (exists_measurableSet_subset_measure_eq) reduces to the R-case by pushing mu forward along the measurable embedding embeddingReal into R (injective, so the pushforward stays finite and atomless), solving there, and pulling the subset back. Both #print axioms clean (propext/Classical.choice/Quot.sound). Classical theorem (Sierpinski 1922; Fremlin, Measure Theory Vol.2, 215D), previously absent from Mathlib. Soundness note: NoAtoms alone is insufficient on a coarse sigma-algebra (countable-cocountable 0/1 counterexample); the standard-Borel hypothesis is exactly what supplies the missing richness.

Sphere invariance of the layer-normalized flow (milestone M3, Phase 1 foundation). MACHINE-CHECKED. sphere_invariant: an integral curve of the tangentially-projected field x-dot = P_x^perp (g t) that starts on the unit sphere S^{d-1} stays on it throughout [0,T], provided the raw radial drift <x t, g t> is uniformly bounded (automatic when g is bounded, e.g. the attention field on the compact sphere). The subtlety is that P_x^perp g is only genuinely tangent ON the sphere: inner_tangentialProjector_left gives <x, P_x^perp w> = <x,w>(1 - ||x||^2), which vanishes exactly at ||x||=1. So u(t)=||x t||^2-1 solves the LINEAR homogeneous ODE u' = c(t) u with u(0)=0, and Grownwall (norm_le_gronwallBound_of_norm_deriv_right_le + gronwallBound_e0_d0) forces u == 0. Grownwall core stated separately as norm_sq_eq_one_of_radial_tangent; derivative-of-norm-squared along a curve as hasDerivAt_norm_sq_sub_one (via HasDerivAt.inner). Mathlib has Picard-Lindelof/Gronwall but no continuity-equation theory, so this in-repo lemma is the geometric well-posedness core the mean-field flow (M3) and the LaSalle/Lyapunov convergence (M6) both rest on. #print axioms clean (propext/Classical.choice/Quot.sound only). Reusable infrastructure; does not yet discharge a paper flow axiom (that is M3 Phase 4).
restrict lemma_B_2 to geodesic balls, not arbitrary sets (M4 fidelity)

Strict Cauchy-Schwarz on the sphere (milestone M4, lemma_B_2 discharge prerequisite). MACHINE-CHECKED. inner_mem_Ioo_of_ne: for distinct unit vectors (x != omega and x != -omega) the inner product <x,omega> lies in the OPEN interval (-1, 1). Proof: two applications of Mathlib's inner_lt_norm_mul_iff_real (strict Cauchy-Schwarz, <x,y> < ||x|| ||y|| iff ||y|| . x != ||x|| . y) -- one to (x, omega) for the upper bound (< 1 iff x != omega) and one to (x, -omega) for the lower bound (> -1 iff x != -omega), with ||x|| = ||omega|| = 1 on the sphere. This is exactly the range hypothesis u = <x,omega> in Ioo (-1) 1 that logistic_flow_reach needs: the logistic coordinate never hits the ODE's fixed points +-1 as long as x avoids the poles +-omega (which are the fixed points of the gated field, tangentialProjector_self). #print axioms clean (propext/Classical.choice/Quot.sound). Reusable geometry prerequisite for the lemma_B_2 dynamical discharge.
Theorem 1.1: for Dirac targets sharing a missing direction, a single Transformer steers N inputs to within eps in W2, with O(d N) switches and norm O(dN/T + log 1/eps).
Theorem 1.2: for targets matchable by transport maps and a shared missing direction, a single Transformer steers N inputs to within eps in W2 with piecewise-constant parameters.

The W2 optimal-transport axiom flip (milestone M2 COMPLETE). MACHINE-CHECKED. Axioms/Wasserstein.lean formerly axiomatised W2 (opaque R-valued) with W2_nonneg / W2_comm / W2_triangle / W2_map_le_L2 / W2_convexCombo_le. Now Axioms.W2 mu nu := (Foundations.W2 mu nu).toReal is a DEFINITION and all five facts are proved THEOREMS over the ENNReal root Kantorovich cost. Because toReal sends top to 0, the honest R statements carry the hypotheses that make the ENNReal->R bridge sound: triangle/convexity carry per-pair finiteness (Foundations.W2 . . != top), discharged at the call sites from bounded/sphere support via W2_ne_top_of_ae_norm_le; the map bound carries measurability + L2-integrability of the displacement. Threading (contained -- prop_2_2 and lemma_5_2 have no consumers, theorem_1_1 does not use the structural facts): (a) prop_2_2 gains a supportedIn mu (sphere d) hypothesis and discharges convexity finiteness via new support-preservation helpers (supportedIn_of_sum_smul: a decomposition piece inherits the whole's support; measureFlow_supportedIn_sphere: the flow keeps the sphere; ae_norm_le_of_supportedIn_sphere), with the dirac targets bounded automatically (R = max 1 ||x k||); (b) the lemma_5_4 axiom is strengthened to expose Measurable psi_eps + L2-integrability (implicit in its integral bound being meaningful), which theorem_1_2 feeds to the map bound; (c) lemma_5_2 (leaf L7) becomes a thin wrapper and is now itself MACHINE-CHECKED. Effect: #print axioms theorem_1_1 / theorem_1_2 no longer list ANY W2/optimal-transport axiom; combined with the M3 flow flip, the ENTIRE infrastructure axiom layer is gone -- the headline theorems rest only on the paper-level mid-level lemmas (cluster_to_point, exists_disentangling_balls, exists_parked_schedule, lemma_5_4). Full audit passes.

Kantorovich-Rubinstein lower bound for W1, one direction (milestone M2). MACHINE-CHECKED. For a 1-Lipschitz test function f, the dual pairing lower-bounds the transport cost of every coupling, hence lower-bounds W1. lipschitz_integral_sub_le_transportCost (per coupling): for a 1-Lipschitz f and a coupling pi of mu,nu with finite cost, integral f dmu - integral f dnu <= integral dist(x,y) dpi. Proof: push f through both marginals (integral_map, so the pairing becomes integral of (f p.1 - f p.2) dpi), then bound the integrand pointwise by dist p.1 p.2 (LipschitzWith.dist_le_mul + le_abs_self) and integrate (integral_mono). ofReal_integral_sub_le_W1 (descent to W1): ENNReal.ofReal (integral f dmu - integral f dnu) <= W1 mu nu, for integrable 1-Lipschitz f -- for each coupling, either cost is infinite (trivial) or finite, in which case dist is pi-integrable (hasFiniteIntegral_iff_ofReal) with integral (transportCost pi).toReal (integral_eq_lintegral_of_nonneg_ae), and ofReal(toReal) <= id closes it. This is the KR direction the paper's Markov bound (Claim 2) uses; it is exactly the content of the axiom W1_ge_of_lipschitz, whose discharge now reduces to threading the ENNReal/R bookkeeping at the use sites. #print axioms clean (propext/Classical.choice/Quot.sound). Requires integrability hypotheses (Integrable f mu/nu) that the general axiom elides; the triangle inequality (gluing) and completeness remain for the full M2.

Optimal transport: couplings and the W1 Kantorovich cost (milestone M2, Phase 0/2 opening). MACHINE-CHECKED. Mathlib v4.31.0 has the Levy-Prokhorov metric but NO optimal transport (no couplings, no Wasserstein, no Kantorovich duality; Axioms/Wasserstein.lean axiomatizes W1/W2). This builds the substrate from scratch on Measure.prod / Measure.fst / Measure.snd. IsCoupling pi mu nu := pi.fst = mu and pi.snd = nu (transport plan with fixed marginals). isCoupling_prod (product/independent coupling for probability measures, via fst_prod/snd_prod), isCoupling_diagonal (the diagonal (id,id)_# mu couples mu with itself -- zero-cost plan), IsCoupling.swap (Prod.swap exchanges marginals, via fst_map_swap/snd_map_swap). transportCost pi := lintegral of edist p.1 p.2 (ENNReal-valued total cost). transportCost_swap (symmetric, via edist_comm), transportCost_diagonal (= 0, via edist_self). W1 mu nu := iInf over couplings of transportCost (ENNReal-valued Kantorovich cost). Metric facts proved unconditionally on the ENNReal lattice: W1_le_transportCost (each plan upper-bounds W1), W1_self_eq_zero (W1 mu mu = 0 via the diagonal), W1_comm (symmetry via swap). #print axioms clean (propext/Classical.choice/Quot.sound). First real slice of M2; the harder facts (Kantorovich-Rubinstein bound needs signed integrals; triangle inequality needs gluing of couplings; completeness) are deferred. Does not yet discharge the W1/W2 axioms (that is the M2 rewiring once KR + triangle land).

Triangle inequality for W1 via gluing of couplings (milestone M2). MACHINE-CHECKED. exists_coupling_transportCost_le (gluing lemma): given a coupling pi1 of (mu,nu) and pi2 of (nu,rho) (probability measures), there is a coupling gamma of (mu,rho) with transportCost gamma <= transportCost pi1 + transportCost pi2. Construction: disintegrate pi2 = nu compProd kappa2 (its conditional z|y, via Measure.disintegrate + condKernel), lift kappa2 to a Y-reading kernel kappa on X x Y (Kernel.comap Prod.snd), form the triple T = pi1 compProd kappa on (X x Y) x Z. The (X,Y)-marginal of T is pi1 (Measure.fst_compProd), the (Y,Z)-marginal collapses to nu compProd kappa2 = pi2 (proved by Measure.ext_of_lintegral + lintegral_compProd + the shared marginal pi1.snd = nu = pi2.fst), and the (X,Z)-marginal gamma is a coupling of (mu,rho) whose cost is bounded via edist x z <= edist x y + edist y z (edist_triangle) and Tonelli. W1_le_transportCost_add (per-coupling): W1 mu rho <= transportCost pi1 + transportCost pi2. W1_triangle: W1 mu rho <= W1 mu nu + W1 nu rho, descending through the two infima by distributing + (ENNReal.iInf_add / add_iInf, valid unconditionally on ENNReal). With W1_self_eq_zero and W1_comm this makes W1 a pseudometric on probability measures. Uses Mathlib's disintegration/kernel machinery (condKernel, compProd, lintegral_compProd) which Wasserstein itself is not built on. #print axioms clean. Completes the W1 metric structure; W2 and the axiom rewiring remain.

Convexity of the W2 (root) Wasserstein distance under mixtures (milestone M2, W2 discharge slice 2). MACHINE-CHECKED. Two mechanical lemmas plus an epsilon-approximation over the infimum. isCoupling_finset_sum_smul: if pi_k couples (P_k, Q_k) for each k, then the mixture sum_k a_k . pi_k couples (sum_k a_k . P_k, sum_k a_k . Q_k) -- the marginal map Prod.fst/snd is additive over the finite sum (Measure.map_sum via sum_fintype) and ENNReal-homogeneous (Measure.map_smul). sqTransportCost_finset_sum_smul: the squared cost is linear in the mixing measure, sqTransportCost (sum_k a_k . pi_k) = sum_k a_k * sqTransportCost (pi_k) (lintegral_finsetSum_measure + lintegral_smul_measure). W2_convexCombo_le: if sum_k a_k = 1 and every W2 (P_k) (Q_k) <= eps, then W2 (sum_k a_k . P_k) (sum_k a_k . Q_k) <= eps. Proof: ENNReal.le_of_forall_pos_le_add reduces to bounding by eps+eta; since W2 is an infimum, W2 (P_k)(Q_k) <= eps < eps+eta yields (iInf_lt_iff) a coupling pi_k of root cost < eps+eta; mix them, and the mixed squared cost sum_k a_k * sqTransportCost(pi_k) <= (eps+eta)^2 * sum_k a_k = (eps+eta)^2 (Minkowski not needed -- the squared cost is already linear in the mixture), so W2(mixture) <= (eps+eta). #print axioms clean. This is the last standalone W2 fact before the all-or-nothing flip of Axioms.W2 to concrete (threading integrability through L7/Prop 2.2/Thm 1.2).

Finiteness of the W2 Wasserstein distance for boundedly-supported probability measures (milestone M2, W2 discharge slice 3: the last foundation blocker before the flip). MACHINE-CHECKED. The ℝ-valued interface Axioms.W2 := (W2 . .).toReal is only faithful where W2 is finite (toReal sends ⊤ to 0, so a hypothesis-free ℝ triangle/convexity fact about a possibly-infinite W2 would be UNSOUND -- exactly the risk the Axioms/Wasserstein.lean comment flags). W2_ne_top_of_ae_norm_le: if mu, nu are probability measures a.e.-supported in the ball of radius R (in particular any measures on the unit sphere, R=1), then W2 mu nu != ⊤. Proof: the product coupling mu.prod nu (isCoupling_prod) moves mass across edist p.1 p.2 = ofReal (dist p.1 p.2) <= ofReal (2R) a.e. (the a.e. norm bounds transported to the product via Measure.quasiMeasurePreserving_fst/snd .ae + filter_upwards + norm_sub_le), so its squared cost is <= ofReal((2R)^2) < ⊤ (lintegral_mono_ae + lintegral_const + measure_univ on the product probability measure), and W2 <= (sqTransportCost)^(1/2) < ⊤ (ENNReal.rpow_ne_top_of_nonneg). #print axioms clean (propext/Classical.choice/Quot.sound). This is the finiteness the ℝ-valued .toReal bridge needs to discharge the W2 triangle/convexity/map axioms honestly; the remaining step is the flip of Axioms.W2 to concrete, threading this finiteness (with the sphere-support facts already in context) through Prop 2.2 / Lemma 5.2 / Thm 1.2.

Quadratic Wasserstein cost W2sq and the map-coupling bound (milestone M2). MACHINE-CHECKED. Mirrors the W1 construction for the quadratic cost: sqTransportCost pi = lintegral of edist(x,y)^2, W2sq mu nu = iInf over couplings of sqTransportCost (the squared W2 distance, ENNReal-valued). Unconditional metric facts: W2sq_le_sqTransportCost, W2sq_self_eq_zero (diagonal coupling), W2sq_comm (swap). W2sq_map_le (Lemma 5.2, squared form): W2sq (mu.map T1) (mu.map T2) <= lintegral of edist(T1 x, T2 x)^2 dmu -- the squared W2 between two pushforwards is bounded by the L2 cost of moving T1 to T2, witnessed by the map coupling (T1,T2)_# mu (marginals via fst_map_prodMk/snd_map_prodMk, cost via lintegral_map). This is the content of the axiom W2_map_le_L2 (the OT fact Lemma 5.2 invokes), in squared ENNReal form. #print axioms clean. Deferred: sqrt to recover W2 itself, the W2 triangle inequality (Minkowski/gluing), W2_convexCombo_le, and the Axioms/Wasserstein.lean rewiring.

Triangle inequality for the W2 (root) Wasserstein distance via Minkowski + gluing (milestone M2, W2 discharge slice 1). MACHINE-CHECKED. W2 mu nu := iInf over couplings of (sqTransportCost pi)^(1/2) -- the root cost (inf of roots, not sqrt of the inf, so the triangle descends through the two infima exactly as for W1). Facts: W2_le_rpow_sqTransportCost (every coupling upper-bounds W2), W2_self_eq_zero (diagonal coupling, 0^(1/2)=0), W2_comm (swap). exists_coupling_rpow_sqTransportCost_le (per-coupling Minkowski/gluing): reuse the gluing triple T of a plan of (mu,nu) and (nu,rho), then bound (int edist(x,z)^2 dT)^(1/2) <= (int edist(x,y)^2)^(1/2) + (int edist(y,z)^2)^(1/2) pointwise via edist_triangle and ENNReal.lintegral_Lp_add_le (Minkowski, p=2). W2_triangle: W2 mu rho <= W2 mu nu + W2 nu rho, descending the two infima (ENNReal.iInf_add/add_iInf). With W2_self_eq_zero + W2_comm, W2 is a pseudometric on probability measures. #print axioms clean. This is the hard blocker of the W2 axiom discharge; deferred still: W2_convexCombo_le and the all-or-nothing flip of Axioms.W2 to concrete (threading integrability through L7/Prop 2.2/Thm 1.2).
